title: Grief Wave Log
description: Track your grief waves and triggers over time. This log helps you identify patterns, notice what helps, and build confidence that waves — even intense ones — do pass. You don't need to log every wave, just the ones that feel significant enough to note.
instructions: When you experience a significant grief wave, log it here as soon as you're able. There are no right or wrong entries. Over time, this log becomes a record of both your pain and your resilience.

fields.label: When did the wave happen?
fields.placeholder: e.g., Tuesday morning, Saturday evening at dinner

fields.label: What triggered it? (if you know)
fields.placeholder: e.g., Heard their favorite song, saw a photo, their birthday approaching, an ordinary moment reminded me of them, no clear trigger

options.label: Ordinary moment (setting the table, driving their route)
No clear trigger — it just arrived

fields.label: What feelings showed up?
fields.placeholder: e.g., Intense sadness, longing, anger, guilt, numbness, a mix of sadness and gratitude

fields.label: How intense was the wave? (0 = mild ripple, 10 = overwhelming)
How did your body react?
fields.placeholder: e.g., Tight chest, tears, exhaustion, heaviness, couldn't move, restless

fields.label: What did you do? What helped (or what might help next time)?
fields.placeholder: e.g., Cried, called a friend, went for a walk, sat with it, did breathing exercises, nothing seemed to help but it passed on its own

fields.label: Roughly how long did the wave last?
fields.placeholder: e.g., A few minutes, about an hour, most of the day
